A.rationale: Five-status model, explicit drain-then-join order, memory-growth-during-hang insight, oracle agreement check, repeats short-circuit; longest but signal-dense and stayed a plan.

B.rationale: Clean, tight four-status plan; nails hang mechanism, spawn safety, in-child timing. Less edge-case depth (no Queue-drain order, no memory note) but crisp.

C.rationale: Failure-mode-first F1-F11, boundary validation, per-cell status insight, fork+spawn contexts, mechanism flag. Slightly looser parent control-flow precision than A.

notes: All three correctly diagnose the pure-Python hang and reject try/except/threads/pool for fresh Process+kill. A wins on depth: explicit Queue-drain ordering, five statuses, memory-OOM-during-hang, oracle check. C nearly ties; B is tightest but shallower.
